Mount ₣BB and resume work on ₢BBAAM (rbrr-depot-identity-collapse). Step 1 landed at commit 3c9d38fb (kindle infrastructure +
  mechanical sweep across ~28 files; build/fast-suite 97/97 green).

  Before proceeding to Step 2, curry the BBAAM-locked design decisions into the paddock so they persist for the rest of the pace.
  Decisions made in conversation, not yet in the paddock:

  - Maximal collapse, not minimal. RBRR drops RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY, RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM and gains
  RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER (format ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle
  constants from (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER).
  - CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-specific). Payor stays on
  RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated, out of scope).
  - Naming shapes: project ${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}; GAR ${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar (note: -gar suffix, not -repository);
  pool ${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool; bucket ${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}. Type markers (d-, b-) and pool suffixes are literals
  inside RBDC to keep rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering.
  - 409 posture deferred. Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker
  collides at project-create against the soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409s left as today (bucket fatal; AR/pools
  idempotent).
  - Mason SA name unchanged (mason-${moniker}); design call deferred.
  - Theurge-side moniker autodetect for pristine fixture cases 2-3: family stems (e.g. pristq, pristg) and the 100000 floor live in
   Rust (rbtdrp_pristine.rs), not in payor code. Six-digit numeric increment.
  - Fact-file shape: <moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT} (extension depot); content is one of
  RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE/BROKEN/DELETE_REQUESTED (tinder constants in rbgc_Constants.sh, already added in Step 1).

  Then propose Step 2 (rbgp_depot_levy reshape) for real-time review before any code change. Step 2's intent per the docket: drop
  the timestamp; drop the CLI <depot-name> positional arg in favor of RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER from kindled regime; use
  RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY, RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM, RBDC_GCS_BUCKET instead of inline computation; remove the
  post-levy banner (already softened to "Depot resources created" in Step 1, but under maximal collapse the operator needs no
  post-levy paste at all — just confirmation). Also remove the differential-furnish skip in rbgp_cli.sh (annotated "Step-2-pending"
   in Step 1) so levy goes through zrbrr_enforce like every other depot operation.

  Steps 3-7 remain in scope under ₢BBAAM (not separate paces): Step 3 unmake reshape, Step 4 depot_list per-moniker fact-file
  emission, Step 5 pristine-fixture autodetect retrofit, Step 6 fast-suite test sweep audit, Step 7 RBS0 + RBSDE banner copy. Pace
  wraps when all six docket items land.

  Honor the paddock's Release-branch execution contract and the "no pre-engineered choices beyond intent" / "real-time review at
  each step before commit" discipline. Each step ends with build green (tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh, tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh,
  tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh) + a notch.

Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapses depot identity in RBRR
  to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and
  related derived names fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp
  from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names,
  displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources
  already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than
  hidden behind a timestamp.
  
  Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent
  only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Reshape `rbgp_depot_levy`: project_id and sub-resource names derive from
    (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER); no timestamp.
  - Collapse RBRR fields: RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM removed,
    replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
  - Reshape `rbgp_depot_list`: emit one fact file per known depot in
    BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (basename keyed on moniker; content = state value).
    State vocabulary in BCG-compliant tinder/kindle constants.
  - Retrofit pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 to autodetect next free moniker
    via depot-list (theurge-side — autodetect is NOT in payor code).
    Six-digit numeric increment starting at the 100000 floor per family
    prefix (e.g., pristq100000, pristg100000).
  - Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
  - RBS0 and operator banner updated to match.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - BURD_OUTPUT_DIR wipe at every tabtarget entry (already enforced by
    bud_dispatch.sh) is the lifecycle anchor: fact files and other artifacts
    are ephemeral; fixturing must read every needed output before invoking
    the next tabtarget.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices
    beyond intent.
  - BCG compliance for new state-value vocabulary (tinder/kindle constants).
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - BBAAK cases 4-5 (smaller follow-on pace, rides on this).
  - Operator-handbook restructuring beyond removing the post-levy
    banner-copy instruction.
  - Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation, not
    a pre-locked redesign.
  - 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call
    at implementation.

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapses depot identity in RBRR
  to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and
  related derived names fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp
  from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names,
  displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources
  already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than
  hidden behind a timestamp.
  
  Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent
  only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Reshape `rbgp_depot_levy`: project_id and sub-resource names derive from
    (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER); no timestamp.
  - Collapse RBRR fields: RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM removed,
    replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
  - Reshape `rbgp_depot_list`: emit one fact file per known depot in
    BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (basename keyed on moniker; content = state value).
    State vocabulary in BCG-compliant tinder/kindle constants.
  - Retrofit pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 to autodetect next free moniker
    via depot-list (theurge-side — autodetect is NOT in payor code).
    Six-digit numeric increment starting at the 100000 floor per family
    prefix (e.g., pristq100000, pristg100000).
  - Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
  - RBS0 and operator banner updated to match.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - BURD_OUTPUT_DIR wipe at every tabtarget entry (already enforced by
    bud_dispatch.sh) is the lifecycle anchor: fact files and other artifacts
    are ephemeral; fixturing must read every needed output before invoking
    the next tabtarget.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices
    beyond intent.
  - BCG compliance for new state-value vocabulary (tinder/kindle constants).
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - BBAAK cases 4-5 (smaller follow-on pace, rides on this).
  - Operator-handbook restructuring beyond removing the post-levy
    banner-copy instruction.
  - Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation, not
    a pre-locked redesign.
  - 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call
    at implementation.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## References
63	
64	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
65	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
66	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
67	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
68	
69	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAM
70	
71	## Character
72	
73	Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapses depot identity in RBRR
74	to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and
75	related derived names fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp
76	from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names,
77	displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources
78	already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAM

## Character

Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapses depot identity in RBRR
to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and
related derived names fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp
from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names,
displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources
already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than
hidden behind a timestamp.

Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent
only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.

## Scope

- Collapse RBRR fields: RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM removed,

- RBS0 and operator banner updated to match.

## Constraints

  are ephemeral; fixturing must read every needed output before invoking
  the next tabtarget.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices
  beyond intent.
- BCG compliance for new state-value vocabulary (tinder/kindle constants).

## Out of scope

- BBAAK cases 4-5 (smaller follow-on pace, rides on this).
- Operator-handbook restructuring beyond removing the post-levy
  banner-copy instruction.
- Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation, not
  a pre-locked redesign.
- 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call
  at implementation.

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. - Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

rbgp_depot_levy() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_depot_name="${1:-}"
  local -r z_region="${RBRR_GCP_REGION}"

  buc_doc_brief "Create new depot infrastructure following RBAGS specification"
  buc_doc_param "depot_name" "Depot name (lowercase/numbers/hyphens, max ${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX} chars)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Validate input parameters'
  test -n "${z_depot_name}" || buc_die "Depot name required as first argument"
  
  if ! [[ "${z_depot_name}" =~ ^[a-z0-9-]+$ ]]; then
    buc_die "Depot name must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens"
  fi
  
  if test "${#z_depot_name}" -gt "${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX}"; then
    buc_die "Depot name must be ${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX} characters or less"
  fi

  # Validate region exists in Artifact Registry locations
  buc_log_args 'Validating region exists in Artifact Registry locations'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor for region validation"

  local -r z_locations_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/projects/${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}/locations"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_locations_url}" "${z_token}" "region_validation"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate region" "region_validation"
  
  local z_valid_regions
  z_valid_regions=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "region_validation" '.locations[].locationId' | tr '\n' ' ') || buc_die "Failed to parse region list"
  
  if ! [[ " ${z_valid_regions} " =~ [[:space:]]${z_region}[[:space:]] ]]; then
    buc_die "Invalid region. Valid regions: ${z_valid_regions}"
  fi

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set"
  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID is not set"
  
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"

  buc_step 'Generate depot project ID'
  local z_timestamp
  date "${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT}" > "${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to generate timestamp"
  z_timestamp=$(<"${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}")
  local -r z_depot_project_id="${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-${z_depot_name}-${z_timestamp}"
  
  if test "${#z_depot_project_id}" -gt 30; then
    buc_die "Generated project ID too long (${#z_depot_project_id} > 30): ${z_depot_project_id}"
  fi
  
  buc_log_args "Generated depot project ID: ${z_depot_project_id}"
  buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_depot_project_id}"

  buc_step 'Create depot project'
  local -r z_create_project_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_create_project.json"

  # OAuth users create projects without parent (per MPCR)
  jq -n \
    --arg projectId "${z_depot_project_id}" \
    --arg displayName "RB Depot ${z_depot_name}" \
    '{
      projectId: $projectId,
      displayName: $displayName
    }' > "${z_create_project_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build project creation body"

  local -r z_create_project_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects"
  rbgu_http_json_lro_ok \
    "Create depot project" \
    "${z_token}" \
    "${z_create_project_url}" \
    "depot_project_create" \
    "${z_create_project_body}" \
    ".name" \
    "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}" \
    "operations/" \
    "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" \
    "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"

  buc_step 'Link billing account'
  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_link.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg billingAccountName "billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}" \
    '{
      billingAccountName: $billingAccountName
    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing link body"

  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/billingInfo"
  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_billing_link" "${z_billing_body}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Link billing account" "depot_billing_link"

  buc_step 'Get depot project number'
  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_project_info"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Get project info" "depot_project_info"
  
  local z_project_number
  # CRM v3 returns project number in name field as "projects/{number}"
  z_project_number=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_project_info" '.name | sub("projects/"; "")') || buc_die "Failed to get project number"
  test -n "${z_project_number}" || buc_die "Project number is empty"

  buc_step 'Enable depot project APIs'
  local -r z_api_services="artifactregistry cloudbuild cloudresourcemanager containeranalysis iam serviceusage storage"
  for z_service in ${z_api_services}; do
    rbgu_api_enable "${z_service}" "${z_depot_project_id}" "${z_token}"
  done

  buc_step 'Create dual worker pools (tether + airgap)'
  local -r z_pool_stem="rbw-${z_depot_name}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_pool_parent="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${z_region}${RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS}"

  # Tether pool (default egress — public internet access)
  local -r z_tether_id="${z_pool_stem}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER}"
  local -r z_tether_create_url="${z_pool_parent}?workerPoolId=${z_tether_id}"
  local -r z_tether_create_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_pool_tether_create.json"

  jq -n --arg machineType "${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}" \
    '{
      privatePoolV1Config: {
        workerConfig: {
          machineType: $machineType
        }
      }
    }' > "${z_tether_create_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build tether pool creation body"

  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_tether_create_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_pool_tether_create" "${z_tether_create_body}"
  local z_tether_create_code
  z_tether_create_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_pool_tether_create") || buc_die "Bad tether pool creation HTTP code"
  case "${z_tether_create_code}" in
    200|201) buc_log_args "Tether pool ${z_tether_id} created" ;;
    409)     buc_log_args "Tether pool ${z_tether_id} already exists (idempotent)" ;;
    *)       buc_die "Failed to create tether pool: HTTP ${z_tether_create_code}" ;;
  esac

  local -r z_airgap_id="${z_pool_stem}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP}"
  local -r z_airgap_create_url="${z_pool_parent}?workerPoolId=${z_airgap_id}"
  local -r z_airgap_create_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_pool_airgap_create.json"

  jq -n --arg machineType "${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}" \
    '{
      privatePoolV1Config: {
        workerConfig: {
          machineType: $machineType
        },
        networkConfig: {

    }' > "${z_airgap_create_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build airgap pool creation body"

  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_airgap_create_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_pool_airgap_create" "${z_airgap_create_body}"
  local z_airgap_create_code
  z_airgap_create_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_pool_airgap_create") || buc_die "Bad airgap pool creation HTTP code"
  case "${z_airgap_create_code}" in
    200|201) buc_log_args "Airgap pool ${z_airgap_id} created" ;;
    409)     buc_log_args "Airgap pool ${z_airgap_id} already exists (idempotent)" ;;
    *)       buc_die "Failed to create airgap pool: HTTP ${z_airgap_create_code}" ;;
  esac

  local -r z_pool_resource="projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${z_region}/workerPools/${z_pool_stem}"
  buc_log_args "Pool stem: ${z_pool_resource}"

  # Note: OAuth Payor doesn't need explicit permissions on depot since it uses user identity
  # Skip Payor permission grants - OAuth user context provides necessary access

  buc_step 'Verify IAM propagation before resource creation'
  local -r z_preflight_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${z_region}/repositories"
  rbgu_poll_until_ok "AR IAM propagation" "${z_preflight_url}" "${z_token}" "iam_preflight"

  buc_step 'Create build bucket'
  local -r z_build_bucket="${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET}-${z_depot_name}-${z_timestamp}"
  local -r z_bucket_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_bucket_create_req.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg name "${z_build_bucket}" \
    --arg location "${z_region}" \
    --arg project "${z_depot_project_id}" \
    '{
      name: $name,
      location: $location,
      storageClass: "STANDARD",
      lifecycle: { rule: [ { action: { type: "Delete" }, condition: { age: 1 } } ] }
    }' > "${z_bucket_req}" || buc_die "Failed to create bucket request JSON"

  local -r z_bucket_create_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b?project=${z_depot_project_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_bucket_create_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_bucket_create" "${z_bucket_req}"
  
  local z_bucket_code
  z_bucket_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_bucket_create") || buc_die "Bad bucket creation HTTP code"
  case "${z_bucket_code}" in
    200|201) buc_log_args "Build bucket ${z_build_bucket} created" ;;
    409)     buc_die "Build bucket ${z_build_bucket} already exists" ;;
    *)       buc_die "Failed to create build bucket: HTTP ${z_bucket_code}" ;;
  esac

  buc_step 'Create container repository'
  local -r z_repository_name="rbw-${z_depot_name}-repository"
  local -r z_parent="projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${z_region}"
  local -r z_create_repo_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_parent}/repositories?repositoryId=${z_repository_name}"
  local -r z_create_repo_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_create_repo.json"
  
  jq -n '{format:"DOCKER"}' > "${z_create_repo_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build create-repo body"

  rbgu_http_json_lro_ok \
    "Create container repository" \
    "${z_token}" \
    "${z_create_repo_url}" \
    "depot_repo_create" \
    "${z_create_repo_body}" \
    ".name" \
    "${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}" \
    "${RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL}" \
    "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" \
    "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"

  buc_step 'Verify IAM API is ready for service account creation'
  local -r z_iam_preflight_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
  rbgu_poll_until_ok "IAM API" "${z_iam_preflight_url}" "${z_token}" "iam_sa_preflight"

  buc_step 'Create Mason service account'
  local -r z_mason_name="${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${z_depot_name}"
  local -r z_mason_display_name="Mason for RB Depot: ${z_depot_name}"
  local -r z_create_sa_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_create_mason.json"
  
  jq -n \
    --arg accountId "${z_mason_name}" \
    --arg displayName "${z_mason_display_name}" \
    '{
      accountId: $accountId,
      serviceAccount: {
        displayName: $displayName
      }
    }' > "${z_create_sa_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build Mason creation body"

  local -r z_create_sa_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_create_sa_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_mason_create" "${z_create_sa_body}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Create Mason service account" "depot_mason_create"
  
  local z_mason_sa_email
  z_mason_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_mason_create" '.email') || buc_die "Failed to get Mason email"

  buc_step 'Verify Mason service account propagation'
  local -r z_mason_sa_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_sa_email}"
  rbgu_poll_until_ok "Mason SA" "${z_mason_sa_url}" "${z_token}" "mason_sa_preflight"

  buc_step 'Configure Mason permissions'
  # Repository admin (AR repo IAM requires email, not numeric ID)
  rbgi_add_repo_iam_role "${z_token}" "${z_depot_project_id}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_region}" "${z_repository_name}" \
    "roles/artifactregistry.writer"

  # Bucket viewer (GCS bucket IAM requires email, not numeric ID)
  rbgi_add_bucket_iam_role "${z_token}" "${z_build_bucket}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "roles/storage.objectViewer"

  # Project viewer
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role "${z_token}" "Grant Mason Project Viewer" "projects/${z_depot_project_id}" \
    "roles/viewer" "serviceAccount:${z_mason_sa_email}" "mason-viewer"

  # Logs writer (for Cloud Build logs to Cloud Logging)
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role "${z_token}" "Grant Mason Logs Writer" "projects/${z_depot_project_id}" \
    "roles/logging.logWriter" "serviceAccount:${z_mason_sa_email}" "mason-logs-writer"

  buc_step 'Provision Cloud Build service agent'
  # The Cloud Build Service Agent (service-{PN}@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com)
  # needs serviceAccountTokenCreator on Mason to impersonate it during builds.create.
  # The service agent is auto-created when the Cloud Build API is enabled; its email is
  # deterministic from the project number. See RBSCIP for the two-SA distinction.
  rbgu_provision_service_agent "cloudbuild" "${z_depot_project_id}" "${z_token}" > /dev/null \
    || buc_die "Failed to provision Cloud Build service agent"
  local -r z_cb_service_agent="service-${z_project_number}@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.${RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN}"
  buc_log_args "CB service agent: ${z_cb_service_agent}"

  buc_step 'Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate Mason'
  rbgi_add_sa_iam_role "${z_token}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_cb_service_agent}" "roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator"

  buc_step 'Update depot tracking'
  zrbgp_depot_list_update || buc_die "Failed to update depot tracking after creation"

  buc_step 'Display depot configuration'
  buc_success 'Depot creation successful'
  buc_info "Depot resources created:"
  buc_bare "  Project:    ${z_depot_project_id}"
  buc_bare "  Region:     ${z_region}"
  buc_bare "  GAR repo:   ${z_repository_name}"
  buc_bare "  Pool stem:  ${z_pool_stem}"
  buc_bare "  Mason SA:   ${z_mason_sa_email}"
  buc_info "Next: mantle Governor for this depot:"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR}"
}

  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "DANGER: Permanently destroy an entire depot infrastructure"

I have full visibility into Step 2's surface now. Here is my proposal for real-time review before I touch any code.

---

## Step 2 — `rbgp_depot_levy` reshape

**Intent:** Levy reads `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` from kindled regime; all derived names come from RBDC kindle constants; no timestamp, no CLI arg, no operator paste afterward.

### Changes I would make

**`Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh` — `rbgp_depot_levy` (lines 568-860):**

1. **Drop the timestamp block** (lines 613-618). `RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT`, `RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN`, etc. — leave the constants alone for now (Mason / Governor / Retriever still timestamp service accounts; that's separate from depot identity).
2. **Drop the CLI positional arg.** Lines 571-587 (depot name capture, validation block, length check) all delete. Region stays as `${RBRR_GCP_REGION}` from regime.
3. **Replace inline derivations with RBDC constants:**
   - line 618 `z_depot_project_id` → use `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` directly throughout (drop the local entirely)
   - line 681 `z_pool_stem` → `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`
   - line 744 `z_build_bucket` → `RBDC_GCS_BUCKET`
   - line 769 `z_repository_name` → `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`
   - line 793 `z_mason_name` → keep as `${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (Mason naming locked unchanged per paddock)
4. **Drop the 30-char project ID guard** (lines 620-622). Regime validation already bounds moniker length 1-26 + `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-` (≤4 chars) → ≤30 by construction.
5. **Trim the post-levy banner** (lines 850-859) to a single confirmation line — operator has nothing to paste; remove `Project / GAR repo / Pool stem / Mason SA` echo block.
6. **Update the predecessor `Next:` hint** at line 564: drop `<depot-name> <region>` args from the example after `rbgp_depot_levy`.
7. **Update `buc_doc_brief` / `buc_doc_param`** at lines 574-575 to reflect zero-argument signature.

**`Tools/rbk/rbgp_cli.sh` (lines 56-65):**

8. **Remove the differential-furnish skip.** Drop the `case` block, leaving `zrbrr_enforce` unconditional. Levy now goes through the same enforcement path as every other depot operation.

### Open questions (asking now, not assuming)

**Q1. `buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_depot_project_id}"` at line 625** — does this stay?

   Under maximal collapse, project_id is purely derivable from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. Theurge fixtures know the moniker (they pick it via autodetect). I'd lean toward **dropping** this `buf_write_fact` call — but BBAAK cases 4-5 (out of scope here) may still need it. I'll keep it unless you say drop.

**Q2. `RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX`** — is this constant still needed elsewhere?

   I see it referenced in this levy, the unmake (line 942 area, `z_ts_suffix_len`), and possibly in rbrr_regime moniker validation. I'll grep before deletion. If unique to levy, remove; if it has other consumers, leave alone in this step.

**Q3. Region validation block** (lines 590-603) — keep as-is?

   This validates `RBRR_GCP_REGION` against AR locations. It's independent of depot identity. I'd keep it untouched in Step 2.

**Q4. `zrbgp_depot_list_update` call at line 848** — any change needed?

   Step 4 reshapes depot_list to per-moniker fact files. For Step 2, I'd leave this call unchanged; Step 4 handles the inside.

### Verification

After the edit:
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` (build green)
- `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh` (unit tests 58/58)
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` (97/97 across the four fast-tier fixtures)
- Notch via `jjx_record` with identity `BBAAM`

lastPrompt: Does Bash Utilities Kit bash exist for general BUK constants?

content.text:
Short answer: **no, there's no general-purpose BUK constants module.** Constants in BUK are scoped per-module — each `bu*` module declares its own tinder/kindle constants:

- `buc_command.sh` — color tinder, log/step formatting kindle
- `buf_fact.sh` — fact-file infrastructure
- `bud_dispatch.sh` — dispatch kindle
- `buh_handbook.sh` — handbook formatting kindle
- `burc_regime.sh`, `burs_regime.sh`, `burd_regime.sh`, `burh_regime.sh` — regime-specific
- `buym_yelp.sh` — yelp wire format

The BCG vocabulary itself (`vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md`) prescribes the **per-module** pattern:
- **Tinder constants** (`PREFIX_lower_name`): top-of-file string literals, available pre-kindle
- **Kindle constants** (`PREFIX_SCREAMING` / `ZPREFIX_SCREAMING`): defined only inside `zprefix_kindle()` with `readonly`

The closest thing to "general" is the practice of cross-module tinder consumption — `buwc_windows.sh` consuming `buhw` tinder, etc. — but that's still each module owning its own namespace.

**Why this matters for your current question:** The depot-state vocabulary (`RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_*`, `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT`) is *RBK*-domain, not BUK-domain. They live correctly in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (RBK's constants module). Nothing in BUK would house them more naturally.

If you were asking about adding a shared facility (like a generic `buf_state_value` helper that takes a state-vocabulary const and a fact-extension const, writes the file, and that any kit could consume), that would be a BUK-level capability worth considering — but it doesn't exist today, and creating one would be a separate pace.

What's the underlying need? Are you considering moving some of the depot-state constants to BUK, or adding a new general facility there?

I read both. Here's my analysis.

**Current slate order** (per `jjx_show BB`):
1. ₢BBAAM rbrr-depot-identity-collapse — **mounted**
2. ₢BBAAO buf-fact-multi-foundation
3. ₢BBAAN cult-verb-foundation
4. ₢BBAAK phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed
5. BBAAC, BBAAE, BBAAF, BBAAG, BBAAH, BBAAI

So BBAAM is currently first, not BBAAO/BBAAN. Either I'm misreading your intent, or the slate doesn't match where the design has moved.

**Dependency structure I now see:**

- **BBAAO → BBAAN → BBAAK**: hard chain. BBAAO establishes `buf_write_fact_multi` + closed extension registry. BBAAN's "observation-fact convention" consumes it (its docket explicitly says "Lands before BBAAN so its observation-fact convention rides on settled BUK ground"). BBAAK is the theurge consumer of BBAAN's cult-verb surface.
- **BBAAO → BBAAM Step 4**: `rbgp_depot_list` per-moniker fact-file emission needs `buf_write_fact_multi`. This is the "another chat" you referenced — BBAAO is that chat.
- **BBAAM Steps 2-3 ↛ anything**: levy/unmake reshape have no upstream dependency.
- **BBAAK ⊃ BBAAM Step 5**: BBAAK collapses cases 2-3 into a single section sharing one throwaway depot per run. The autodetect-next-free-moniker logic that BBAAM Step 5 was going to add becomes either irrelevant (one throwaway depot, not a family scan) or absorbed into BBAAK directly.

**Do I agree with the design?** Yes — the foundation-first ordering (BBAAO → BBAAN → BBAAK) is sound. Each consumer pace lands on settled ground rather than chasing a moving target. BBAAO's BUK/RB boundary discipline (extensions registry stays in `buf_fact.sh`; RB extensions stay in `rbgc_Constants.sh`) is the right cut.

**Should we reslate BBAAM?** I think yes, but split rather than wholesale-defer.

Two reasons it's not a clean defer:
- **Levy is half-broken right now.** Step 1 landed in 3c9d38fb but the function still has the CLI-arg + timestamp shape; the post-levy banner I'd remove in Step 2 was already softened in Step 1. Leaving levy unusable until BBAAO+BBAAN+BBAAK all land (a substantial wall-clock) is operator-unfriendly.
- **Step 5 likely becomes obsolete.** BBAAK's "one throwaway depot per run" supersedes "autodetect next free moniker per family stem." Same for the test fixture autodetect work.

**Recommendation: split BBAAM into two paces:**

- **BBAAM (this pace, narrowed)** — Steps 2, 3 only. Reshape `rbgp_depot_levy` and `rbgp_depot_unmake` to use RBDC + `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; remove differential-furnish skip; trim post-levy banner. Closes the broken levy/unmake state. Land now.
- **BBAAM' (new pace, slated AFTER BBAAK)** — Steps 4, 6, 7. depot_list reshape using BBAAO's `buf_write_fact_multi` and `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT`; fast-suite test sweep audit; RBS0 + RBSDE banner copy. Step 5 drops (subsumed by BBAAK).

That gives slate order: **BBAAM (narrowed) → BBAAO → BBAAN → BBAAK → BBAAM' → BBAAC → ...**

Alternative if you want maximum cleanliness: defer BBAAM entirely (slate it after BBAAK), accept that levy/unmake stay in their current half-broken state for the foundation-pace duration. The cost is real but bounded — there's no production user of pristine-lifecycle right now.

What's your call: split, or full defer?
